title: Type 3 Control Channel
established: Separating control from data — evolved into unified message protocol

title: Push-Phase Memoization
established: RESOLVED signal for transitive skip — carried forward

title: Python Port Strategy
established: Protocol classes, unlimited bitmask, per-subgraph locks, core sync

title: Lazy Tier 2
established: get() doesn't guarantee freshness — evolved into status-based trust

title: Vision: LLM Actuator
established: Three-layer vision, Graph as universal output — directly triggered GraphReFly

title: Python-Specific Lessons Carried Forward
established: Typed Protocol classes > integer tags; unlimited-precision bitmask; per-subgraph RLock; core 100% synchronous; superset deps at construction; context managers; free-threaded Python 3.14 readiness

title: Unified Spec: Protocol, Single Primitive, Graph Container
topic: Designing the GraphReFly unified cross-repo spec through a 7-step process. Radical simplification from callbag-recharge's 6 primitives + 4 callbag types to 1 primitive (node) + unified message format.
decisions: One primitive: node(deps?, fn?, opts?) — behavior from configuration, sugar constructors for readability
Unified message format: always [[Type, Data?], ...], 9 types, no channel separation
Unified node interface: .get() (cached, never errors), .status, .down(), .up(), .unsubscribe(), .meta
Meta as companion stores — each key subscribable, replaces all with_*() wrappers
No separate Knob/Gauge/Inspector — describe() + observe() on Graph
Pure wire edges — no transforms, everything is a node
Colon namespace — "system:payment:validate"

title: Built-in ABAC: Actor, Guard, Policy Builder
topic: Designing built-in access control for GraphReFly that replaces external authz libraries. The graph is the single enforcement point.
decisions: Three primitives: Actor context (who), capability guard (may they), scoped introspection (what can they see)
policy() declarative builder — CASL-style allow()/deny() DX, zero dependencies
Attribution pulled to Phase 1.5 — node.last_mutation records { actor, timestamp }
CASL rejected as dependency
Web3 identity maps cleanly — wallet signatures, x402 proofs, ERC-8004 agent IDs all produce actors
roadmap_impact: New Phase 1.5 (Actor & Guard), expanded Phase 1.6 (tests), Phase 5.4 accepts actor?, Phase 6 simplified

title: Python companion to TS audit program
topic: Same audit-plan.md program; Python-specific follow-ups (batch drain vs TS, xfailed operator tests, SQLite checkpoint concurrency, type-alias hygiene).
decisions: Python-specific batch drain vs TS inconsistency documented
xfailed operator tests for Py gaps
SQLite checkpoint concurrency considerations
Type-alias hygiene follow-ups

title: Tier 2 Operator Parity: forwardInner, nonlocal, sample Architecture
topic: Cross-repo parity fixes for three divergences in Python's Tier 2 operators. All code changes are in this repo; TS was already correct.
decisions: _forward_inner emitted flag — prevents double-DATA when inner emits during subscribe
nonlocal replaces [value] list-boxing — idiomatic Python 3 across all 18 operators
sample rewritten to dep+onMessage — eliminates mirror node
Tier 2 regression testing is the top testing priority

title: Demo & Test Strategy: 4 Demos, Inspection-as-Test-Harness, Foreseen Building Blocks
topic: Demo and test strategy for Phase 4 domain layers. Python-specific: Pyodide/WASM lab, thread-safety as first-class test axis.
decisions: Four demos mirroring TS topology using Python APIs (text/table output in Pyodide REPL)
Scenario tests mirror TS demo AC lists, adapted for snake_case and context managers
Adversarial tests include thread-safety dimension
Foreseen building blocks include thread-safe mock LLM and Pyodide-safe fallbacks

title: Universal Reduction Layer: Massive Info → Actionable Items via LLM-Composable Reactive Graphs
topic: Generalizing GraphReFly to a universal reactive reduction layer. Python companion to canonical TS session.
decisions: Adapter ecosystem advantages: confluent-kafka, clickhouse-connect, boto3, redis-py
Per-subgraph RLock critical for high-throughput reduction; free-threaded Python 3.14 enables true parallelism
OTel bridge: from_otel() should accept opentelemetry-sdk interfaces
multiprocessing-based sharded_graph for scaling beyond single process

title: Adoption Blockers: Memory Footprint, DAG-CBOR Codec, Tiered Representation, NodeV0 Promotion
topic: Python companion: DAG-CBOR, tiered representation, memory tuning, delta checkpoints, NodeV0 promotion.
decisions: DAG-CBOR via cbor2 (C-accelerated) or dag-cbor for strict IPLD interop; zstandard for compression
Python per-node overhead ~1200-1500 bytes — __slots__, lazy meta, numpy struct-of-arrays even more impactful
Per-subgraph RLock enables per-subgraph dormant eviction without global locks
Arrow/Parquet ecosystem advantage: pyarrow, polars for cold-tier snapshots
roadmap_impact: GraphCodec interface, delta checkpoints, lazy hydration, NodeV0 promotion to Phase 3.x
